hypothesis: Metal lattices selected by a machine-learning model trained on template-substituted prototypes—using features including strong interstitial electron localization—will yield thermodynamically stable metal superhydrides upon DFT validation, and those lattices that also exhibit three-dimensional electronic connectivity, high hydrogen content, and substantial hydrogen-derived density of states at the Fermi level will display strong electron-phonon coupling and high superconducting transition temperatures under high pressure.
